channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Delhi government to pilot youth-led civic tech solutions from Delhi Next hackathon
item.description: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has announced that several innovative ideas emerging from the 'Delhi Next – Code, Create & Change' hackathon will be taken forward for pilot implementation by the gove...

item.title: Analysis of MAGA Reaction to Supreme Court Birthright Citizenship Ruling
item.description: The article analyzes the political and ideological reaction from MAGA-aligned figures following a Supreme Court decision that reaffirmed the long-standing interpretation of the 14th Amendment’s birthr...

item.title: Star Magic artists talk about their aspirations and future goals
item.description: Star Magic talents Francine Diaz, Lella Ford, Rave Victoria, along with other emerging artists under ABS-CBN’s Star Magic, took part in a recent feature where they opened up about their personal dream...

item.title: South Africa records highest June vehicle sales in 19 years amid strong fleet demand
item.description: South Africa’s new vehicle market delivered its strongest June performance in nearly two decades, with domestic sales reaching 54,482 units, according to industry body Naamsa. This marks the highest J...

item.title: Kenneth Okonkwo Explains ADC’s Choice of Vice Presidential Candidate and South-East Exclusion
item.description: Kenneth Okonkwo, a prominent chieftain of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), has explained the reasoning behind the party’s decision regarding the choice of vice presidential candidate for the 202...

item.title: Kenya Police Clear Barricades After Morning Gridlock
item.description: Police in Kenya have cleared barricades erected by suspected thugs near Pangani, restoring traffic flow on the Thika Superhighway after a morning disruption. The incident, which occurred on Friday, sa...

item.title: Thousands of migrants transferred to new Musina repatriation centre amid food shortages
item.description: Thousands of migrants who had been stranded at the Musina showgrounds in Limpopo are being relocated to a newly established repatriation facility at Proefplaas Farm, approximately 20km outside the tow...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania launches third fintech regulatory sandbox cohort; applications open until 31 July 2026 alongside wider Africa finance updates
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BOT) has officially opened applications for the third cohort of its Fintech Regulatory Sandbox, giving financial institutions, mobile money operators, electronic money issuers, a...

item.title: Opinion on Proposed U.S. Defense Legislation and U.S.–Israel Military Cooperation
item.description: The article presents a strongly critical opinion regarding proposed provisions in the U.S. National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), specifically a section referenced as Section 219, which the author...

item.title: Suspected avian influenza case detected in New South Wales
item.description: The first suspected case of highly pathogenic bird flu has been confirmed in New South Wales, marking a significant biosecurity development for the state. According to the report, authorities have ide...

item.title: Kenneth Okonkwo Reacts to Defamation Suit Filed by Peter Obi, Cites Past Support
item.description: A chieftain of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Kenneth Okonkwo, has reacted strongly to a legal action brought against him by the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC)...

item.title: Ed Gamble reflects on the songs and artists that shaped his musical life
item.description: Comedian and podcaster Ed Gamble looks back on the music that has shaped his life in a feature exploring his personal ‘soundtrack’. He recalls early exposure to David Bowie, particularly ‘Life On Mars...

item.title: Tanzania urges phased approach to SADC Development Fund implementation amid funding concerns
item.description: Tanzania has called for a careful and well-structured implementation of the Southern African Development Community (SADC) Regional Development Fund, stressing the need for a phased approach due to fin...

item.title: Search ends in tragedy after 7-year-old boy found dead in Hamilton conservation area on Canada Day
item.description: A search and rescue operation in Hamilton, Ontario, ended in tragedy after a 7-year-old boy who went missing on Canada Day was found dead in a local conservation area. According to the report, the chi...

item.title: Guide to Heathrow Airport parking discounts, offers and savings for July 2026
item.description: This article from The Independent provides a detailed overview of how travellers can save money on Heathrow Airport parking in July 2026. It focuses on available discount codes, promotional offers, an...

item.title: China urges India-Japan partnership not to be directed against its interests amid supply chain cooperation
item.description: China has stated that the growing strategic partnership between India and Japan should not be directed against its interests, following a series of agreements signed between New Delhi and Tokyo to str...

item.title: Kenyan businessman recounts losses and assault after returning from South Africa amid xenophobic tensions
item.description: A Kenyan Somali businessman, Ali Sambul, has shared his difficult experience after returning home from South Africa, where he had lived and worked for several years. Speaking upon his arrival in Kenya...

item.title: Sowore alleges long-standing political persecution over decades of activism and arrests
item.description: Omoyele Sowore, the presidential candidate of the African Action Congress (AAC), has alleged that successive Nigerian governments have been targeting him since he was 18 years old due to his activism ...

item.title: Centre seeks responses from Telegram and Signal over username-based features amid privacy and fraud concerns
item.description: The Union government of India has issued notices to messaging platforms Telegram and Signal, asking them to explain the safeguards they have in place to prevent misuse of username-based communication ...

item.title: Winter travel in South Africa: embracing slower journeys and reflective experiences
item.description: The article explores the unique character of winter travel, focusing on how the colder season encourages a slower, more reflective way of experiencing places in South Africa and beyond. It describes w...
